data.description: Learn how to set up your Telegram bot with TBL (Tele Bot Lang) codes for the first time. Complete step-by-step guide with examples and img.

    h3 { font-size:1.4rem; color:#0088cc; margin-top:40px; margin-bottom:15px; }
    p { margin-bottom:15px; color:#333; }
    ul { margin:15px 0 20px 20px; }
    li { margin-bottom:15px; }
    pre { background:#f9f9f9; padding:15px; border-radius:10px; overflow-x:auto; font-size:0.95rem; margin:15px 0; border:1px solid #eee; }
    code { color:#d63384; }

    .important { font-weight:600; color:#d63384; }
    .img-step {
      max-width: 50%;
      height: auto;
      margin: 10px auto 25px;
      border-radius: 10px;
      border: 1px solid #eee;
      display: block;
    }
    a { color:#0088cc; text-decoration:none; font-weight: bold; }

      /* Use transform to displace the element its full height, effectively pushing the link out of
       * view, as it's already appended at the bottom. Since the link does not have fixed

<header id="header">
  <div class="container nav-container">
    <a href="/" class="logo">
      <img src="/assets/img/logo.png" alt="TeleBotHost Logo">
      TeleBotHost
    </a>

    </nav>

<section>
  <h2>Setting Up Your Bot with TBL Codes</h2>
  <p class="subtitle">A complete beginner-friendly guide to add your first TBL (Tele Bot Lang) code to your Telegram bot.</p>

  <h3>Introduction to TBL</h3>
  <p>TBL (Tele Bot Lang) is a lightweight scripting language designed for Telegram bot development. Its JavaScript-inspired syntax and built-in functions make creating bots fast and easy, without external dependencies.</p>

  <h3>Key Features of TBL</h3>
  <ul>
    <li>Simplified command-based structure for bots</li>
    <li>Predefined variables for user, chat, and message context</li>
    <li>Built-in API methods for sending messages, keyboards, and inline responses</li>
    <li>Support for HTTP requests, global variables, and modular extensions</li>
    <li>Supports both synchronous (80%) and asynchronous (20%) execution with <code>await</code> and <code>Promise</code></li>
    <li>Responds to specific command triggers rather than general events</li>
  </ul>
<ins class="adsbygoogle" style="display: block; height: 226px;" data-ad-format="fluid" data-ad-layout-key="-fb+5w+4e-db+86" data-ad-client="ca-pub-2719295794573158" data-ad-slot="8786409519" data-adsbygoogle-status="done"><div id="aswift_1_host" style="border: none; height: 226px; width: 910px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; position: relative; visibility: visible; background-color: transparent; display: inline-block;"><iframe id="aswift_1" name="aswift_1" style="left:0;position:absolute;top:0;border:0;width:910px;height:226px;min-height:auto;max-height:none;min-width:auto;max-width:none;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-popups allow-popups-to-escape-sandbox allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation" width="910" height="226" frameborder="0" marginwidth="0" marginheight="0" vspace="0" hspace="0" allowtransparency="true" scrolling="no" allow="attribution-reporting; <script type="text/javascript">
     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
</script>

  <h3>Step 1: Access Your Bot</h3>
  <p>Ensure your bot is already added to TeleBotHost. Go to the <b>Bot List</b> and click on your bot's name to open its management panel.</p>
  <img src="/assets/img/step1-code.jpg" alt="Bot List" class="img-step">

  <h3>Step 2: Open the Commands Tab</h3>
  <p>Navigate to the <b>Commands</b> tab. You should see the <code>/start</code> command created in your first tutorial.</p>
  <img src="/assets/img/step2-code.jpg" alt="Commands Tab" class="img-step">

  <h3>Step 3: Open the Code Editor</h3>
  <p>Click on the command name (<code>/start</code>) to open the TBL code editor.</p>
  <img src="/assets/img/step3-code.jpg" alt="Code Editor Open" class="img-step">

  <h3>Step 4: Add Your First TBL Code</h3>
  <p>Type the following simple code in the editor to send a message:</p>
  <pre><code>Bot.sendMessage("Hello! This message is from Bot codes");</code></pre>
  <img src="/assets/img/step4-code.jpg" alt="Add TBL Code" class="img-step">
<ins class="adsbygoogle" style="display: block; text-align: center; height: 280px;" data-ad-layout="in-article" data-ad-format="fluid" data-ad-client="ca-pub-2719295794573158" data-ad-slot="8142878854" data-adsbygoogle-status="done"><div id="aswift_2_host" style="border: none; height: 280px; width: 910px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; position: relative; visibility: visible; background-color: transparent; display: inline-block;"></div></ins>
<script type="text/javascript">
     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
</script>

  <h3>Step 5: Save Your Code</h3>
  <p>Click the <b>Save</b> button at the bottom to store your changes.</p>
  <h3>Step 6: Test Your Bot</h3>
  <p>Go to Telegram, open your bot, and send <code>/start</code>. Your bot should reply with the message you added in TBL.</p>
  <img src="/assets/img/step5-code.jpg" alt="Test Bot" class="img-step">

  <h3>Congratulations!</h3>
  <p>🎉 You have successfully added your first TBL code to your bot. Explore the <a href="/docs">documentation</a> to unlock more commands, inline keyboards, and advanced bot features.</p>
